competitive_position: GEP Worldwide was the single live peer threat (Feb–April 2026, priced 50% below Zycus per 3 April note), but Mair selected Zycus as preferred vendor 3 April, issued LOI 8 May, and signed 12 June — GEP is OUT, competition is over. Coupa (overcomplicated, abandoned implementation), SAP Ariba (unused licence write-off), and Kissflow (BPM build-vs-buy, too slow) are historical context, not active threats. Zycus won on speed to value (pre-built SAP/Yardi connectors vs GEP's custom build cycle), unified three-ERP experience under SSO, regional Middle East presence + local team, phased rollout flexibility, and Saas-fit contracting template. No active competitors as of 1 July 2026; deal is closed.

champion.source: Vishal contact role + Avoma touchpoints 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8; Lovemer contact role + touchpoints 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14; gap: no evidence of anyone coaching Zycus on internal politics or defending Zycus vs GEP post-3 April selection
champion.summary: NO active internal champion with advocacy evidence. Vishal Kapil (Group CIO/CTO, Decision Maker) is a credible technical validator with prior Zycus experience (Adidas 2016) and joined all major discovery/demo sessions, but he is NOT an advocate — no evidence he is selling Zycus internally, coaching Dan/Zycus on politics, or defending Zycus vs GEP when the competitive conversation happened. Lovemer Raymundo (Procurement Coordinator) is the operational coordinator (appears on 11 of 15 calls, most frequent Mair attendee) and will be the Mair-side project lead for Phase 1, but he is not a decision-maker or advocate. Ranjit Warang (Senior Manager - SAP MM & SD, Evaluator) led requirements gathering but no advocacy evidence. Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli (Director of Procurement / CPO) is the Economic Buyer and commercial authority (drove pricing, signed contract 12 June), fully engaged and bought-in, but he is making the decision, not helping Zycus win it — he is the EB, not a coach. The deal closed WITHOUT a classic champion (internal seller who coaches Zycus, defends against competition, and navigates escalations), which is a structural risk for Phase 1 execution and Phases 2–3: if Phase 1 delivery hits a snag (integration delay, user adoption issue, scope creep), Zycus has no internal coach to navigate escalation or defend vs potential GEP re-engagement (GEP was 50% Zycus pricing and could resurface if Phase 1 falters). Status: WEAK/NONE. Recommended move: build Lovemer into an active champion during Phase 1 by giving him early wins (visibility dashboards, sourcing success, DOA audit trail), positioning him as the Mair-side project lead and the hero of the rollout, and establishing a direct line to Hrishi (implementation lead) and Dan. Schedule quarterly exec check-ins with Mazin and Vishal to surface issues before they escalate.
champion.champion: Vishal Kapil (Group CIO/CTO, Decision Maker contact role) is the closest to a champion: technical validator with prior Zycus experience (Adidas 2016), credible internal voice, joined all major discovery/demo sessions (8 of 15 touchpoints). However, Vishal is a VALIDATOR, not an advocate — no evidence of internal selling, coaching, or defending Zycus vs GEP.

recommended_moves: Schedule Phase 1 kickoff meeting with Mazin, Vishal, Lovemer, and Ranjit within 7 days to confirm Phase 1 scope, timeline, and integration priorities
Front-load Yardi ERP integration workshops (Yardi API access, SAP CPI connector setup, data mapping) in first 4 weeks of Phase 1 to de-risk 6-month go-live
Build Lovemer Raymundo (Procurement Coordinator) into an active champion by giving him Phase 1 early wins and positioning him as the Mair-side project lead

Proc team are chasing legal, hoping to have it resolved Thurs latest, they are not aware of any issues/showstoppers - I reiterated the call option with Zoheb and they will push their own legal again to arrange (if needed).... The CPO/signatory is off today so no answer on the signing ceremony with Carl, I am pushing for Thurs or Fri, they understand the urgency of booking your travel and will update me tomorrow on his return- if they agree to sig Friday it's a good sign for the contract negotiations/T4C.<span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0); font-size: 11pt;">1st June - No update last week as the team were all off for the Eid public holiday, caught up this morning for an update: they have a meeting with Legal today to get an update on progress, they have agreed to a direct call with Zoheb and their legal (Omar) early this week to resolve any outstanding issues and move towards signature. <span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0); font-size: 11pt;">25th May - MSA and Order 1 close to completion with Mair legal- will have full update by Monday 25th May 19th - Redlines on MSA received today, (thanks to Zoheb for a quick turnaround) - some back and forth on clauses and a negotiation needed on a termination for convenience - reverted.May 18th - Following emergency below, Mair head of legal has agreed to continue reviewing our paper, feedback/redlines expected Tues 19th or Weds 20th (Zoheb continues to review theirs in parallel).May 14th - Lovemer has a meeting on 14th with Omar (Mair Head of Legal) to discuss the MSA/OF will update us asap afterwards with feedback.May 12th - Reminded them for the SOW workshop - Hrishi +1 (& Dan) will travel and hold onsite to minimise any risk, date TBC.May 11th - Mair legal are reviewing Zycus MSA and hopeful to agree on using our paper- will know in next 2 days (this will greatly accelerate signing)No immediate blockers in Ts & Cs.May 8th - LOI received - likely MSA/Order 1 sign off "before Eid break" (i.e. before 26th May) - pushing for sooner.May 7th - All issues resolved, some back & forth on implementation phasing, but nothing to worry about, verbal comment from Head of Proc Ops: "Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week".May 6th - Meeting below held: (positive session), mostly based on clarifications of: a) implementation invoicing across phases (resolved), b) number of onsite teams (resolved), c) travel budget (Outstanding - they are asking us to swallow it all - we are pushing back, to split 50/50 as this stage - action for them to confirm, otherwise potentially intro Carl to Mazin-CPO), d) we also raised the legal paperwork, their policy/preference is their own standard template, (Outstanding - not a showstopper, but made a case that our paper is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose and faster/easier to edit than rewriting theirs, action for them to confirm). Intention is to resolve these issues by the end of the week, ideally then to submit LOI by EOW (Fri 8th May) and signed Contracts before Eid public holiday (w/c Mon 25th May).May 4th - Provided some clarifications (Implementation onsite support, payment terms, travel) - they followed up with a request for a call on Weds 6th to discuss: Phased Implementation plan timeline, Payment Terms and Travel fees<span style="font-size: 14.6667px; color: rgb(36, 36, 36); background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"> - Carl, Tanya & Hrishi are joining, (also meeting on 5th to strategize).30th April/May 1st - Some back & forth clarifications, nothing concerning.29th April - Comment from CPO after a nudge: "Still going through some internal review process. Hope to close this asap".27th April - Requested (already sent) and resent MSA & Oder Form templates for internal review, also completed a vendor registration form and provided documentation (Trade Licence, TRN, Bank Details etc.), made clear that using our paper is preferred but not a showstopper.24th April - They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week, looking like they will push for their paper, asked to discuss with CPO as ours is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose, faster, mutual, no risk etc. etc.23rd April - Liaised with Carl offered some BAFO concessions (through discount options held over for this phase e.g. Publicity 5%), license pricing mostly protected in return for: a) min 5 years, b) strong reference-ability/PR and c) speed of decision (end May signature latest) - BAFO & MSA/Oder Form templates shared - Next Step: requested a call with CPO Fri/Sat/Sun to discuss. 21st April - F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years (started the meeting committing to only 3), will be a reference customer and a quick decision - followed up with an mail confirming & asking for closing BAFO price with preference to Zycus- some niggly terms we should be able to navigate, due Thursday, with formal decision expected by mid-May, liaising with Carl to review.16th April - Next step is a face-to-face meeting in Abu Dhabi to discuss commercials & implementation, Dan & Tanya will be on-site with Hrishi dialling in - will synch with Carl prior to discuss commercial tactics.9th April - Clarifications and revised implementation pricing (now a longer phased approach) hand delivered - retained original SaaS pricing in expectation of a negotiation (champion tip), made clear the assumption is a single phased contract, digital copies also added to their Showpad link. NOTE: Some issues where we were not able to meet their requirements/match GEP i.e. RTO/RPO (concerns due to current war situation and AWS taken out in Dubai & Bahrain) - champion thinks it won't be a showstopper.6th April - Revised phasing and further formal clarifications received - response to be hard copy/hand delivered to Abu Dhabi office Thurs latest.3rd April - Positive clarifications call, some push to improve RTO/RPO and uptime to match the competition, but doesn't appear to be a showstopper, also looking to move away from big bang to a phased approach focussing on priority modules e.g. Sourcing first, not clear economic impact yet, but have indicated it will still be a single contract - follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP, next steps they are going to confirm on 6th/7th what their phasing plan will be and we will need to review/update commercials accordingly - note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp.1st April - Clarification responses run-through call scheduled for Fri 3rd April.30th Mar - Clarifications received and responses submitted.25th March - Nudged Lovemer for an update on evaluation & timelines, Vishal (CTO) called later to clarify some RFP response elements, potentially a good sign as he said he is not supposed to do it and it's against the proc policies to give us an advantage, will clarify and respond asap today/tomorrow.6th March - RFP Technical & Commercial response hand delivered to HQ in Abu Dhabi,<span style="background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255);"> response clarifications is next step. (Commercial positioned as a budgetary estimate as we had to guesstimate many data points, also held back on some discounts in anticipation of further negotiation).26th Feb - Clarifications received (still many gaps, will need to guesstimate and make caveats in pricing) - deadline extended to 6th March - still expecting a hand delivered hard copy to Abu Dhabi.10th Feb - RFP for S2P received - deadline is 25th Feb, clarifications 18th.... currently requires hard copy submission to Abu Dhabi, requested Showpad or alternative.SEE ATTACHED NOTES FOR HISTORY REMOVED DUE TO LACK OF SPACE

sources: MEDDPICC_2_0__c 'Who_owns_the_budget__c': 'CFO/Finance via Board approval process; Vishal/Mazin recommendation' (CRM-entered 20 Feb 2026)
Mazin contact role: Director of Procurement, primary commercial negotiator and contract signatory
Avoma 21 April 2026 F2F (touchpoint 9, notes): Mazin drove pricing negotiation, agreed to 5-year term + reference in exchange for Year 1 discount
Next Step timeline: Mazin signed contract 12 June 2026, confirmed signature same day ('he has confirmed he will be signing it today. SIGNED SIGNATURE PAGE RECEIVED')
Avoma 6 May 2026 call (touchpoint 10): Mazin escalated travel-budget impasse internally, confirmed LOI target by Friday 8 May
Avoma 10 June 2026 legal call (touchpoint 13): Mazin (CPO) attended legal-to-legal session, was 'VERY firm' on arbitration clause (board resolution), drove final contract resolution
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: CFO/Finance controls budget via board approval process, with Vishal Kapil (Group CIO/CTO, Procurement head) and Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli (Director of Procurement, CPO) as the recommending stakeholders and deal drivers (source: MEDDPICC_2_0__c 'Who_owns_the_budget__c', CRM-entered 20 Feb 2026). Mazin is the commercial and contracting signatory: he led pricing negotiations (21 April F2F meeting, pushed for Year 1 sweetener in exchange for 5-year term and reference-ability), escalated travel-budget impasse to SVP level (6 May call), drove LOI issuance (8 May), and signed the final contract (12 June signature page received, source: Next Step). Vishal (CIO/CTO) is the technical validator and process owner, joined all major demos and workshops (26 Nov, 9–12 Dec 2025, 3 April 2026 ERP integration call), but Mazin is the commercial authority. The CFO/Finance approval gate was confirmed on multiple calls (6 May: 'board approval process', 21 April: Mazin stated 'approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week'), and LOI was issued 8 May post-board sign-off. EB is fully engaged and bought-in: Mazin personally negotiated pricing, defended Zycus template vs Mair standard (May–June legal calls), and drove signature despite 11th-hour legal pushback. Access: direct, active, commercial decision-maker throughout.
